text: Large Language Models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence trained on vast amounts of text data from the internet, books, and articles. They work by predicting the next word in a sentence based on patterns learned from this data. When you give an LLM a prompt, it generates a response by analyzing the words you've used and continuing naturally. This process, called prompt engineering, helps you get better results by crafting clear questions. LLMs can write essays, answer questions, and even translate languages. However, they have limits. They don't truly understand meaning, may produce incorrect facts, and can be biased if their training data is flawed.
